14.reason: Error: Should use %c to read a character, not %d. Using %d will read an integer instead of the newline character, causing incorrect input parsing.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should use %c to read a character, not %d. Using %d will read an integer instead of the newline character, causing incorrect input parsing.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should use %c to read a character, not %d. Using %d will read an integer instead of the newline character, causing incorrect input parsing.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: should use "%c" to read a character, not "%d"
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Compilation error: %d expects argument of type int*, but c0 is char* causing type mismatch.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Format specifier mismatch. %d expects an int pointer, but &c0 is a char pointer. Should be %c to correctly read a character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Format specifier '%d' expects an 'int *', but '&c0' is a 'char *'. This causes a type mismatch compilation error/warning and undefined behavior. It should be '%c'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using %d to read into a char variable causes undefined behavior; should use %c instead]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect format specifier: using %d to read a char leads to undefined behavior. Should use %c instead.]
